You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 315e75d0).

## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

Which will violate the max-line-length rule.

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used:
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
```
[*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 120
```
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint 0.48.1, CLI

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import mu.KotlinLogging
 5→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6→
 7→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10→
11→/**
12→ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13→ */
14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15→
16→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17→private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→
39→                else ->
40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {
44→                            if (!it.isValid) {
45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }
59→        },
60→        propertyWriter = { property ->
61→            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63→            } else {
64→                property.toString()
65→            }
66→        },
67→    )
68→
69→private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70→    when (this) {
71→        CodeStyleValue.android -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72→        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
73→        CodeStyleValue.official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
74→        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
75→        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
76→    }
77→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

 4→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
 5→
 6→class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleTest {
 7→    private val functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule() }
 8→
 9→    @Test
10→    fun `Given a function signature without whitespace between the closing parenthesis and the colon of the return type then do not reformat`() {
11→        val code =
12→            """
13→            fun foo(): String = "some-result"
14→            """.trimIndent()
15→        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()

17→

23→            """.trimIndent()
24→        val formattedCode =
25→            """
26→            fun foo(): String = "some-result"
27→            """.trimIndent()

59→        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
60→            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
61→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
62→    }
63→
64→    @Test
65→    fun `Given a function signature with multiple spaces between the colon and the return type then reformat`() {
66→        val code =
67→            """
68→            fun foo():  String = "some-result"
69→            """.trimIndent()
70→        val formattedCode =
71→            """
72→            fun foo(): String = "some-result"
73→            """.trimIndent()
74→        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
75→            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
76→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
77→    }
78→
79→    @Test
80→    fun `Given a function signature with a new line between the colon and the return type then reformat`() {
81→        val code =
82→            """
83→            fun foo():
84→                String = "some-result"
85→            """.trimIndent()
86→        val formattedCode =
87→            """
88→            fun foo(): String = "some-result"
89→            """.trimIndent()
90→        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
91→            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
92→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
93→    }
94→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ANNOTATION
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ANNOTATION_ENTRY
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LPAR
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId

 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY

 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 28→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 29→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lineIndent
 30→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 31→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 32→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 33→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 34→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 35→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 36→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 37→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 38→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 39→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 40→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.FunctionSignatureRule.FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.always
 41→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.FunctionSignatureRule.FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.default
 42→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.FunctionSignatureRule.FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.multiline
 43→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 44→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafElement
 47→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 48→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.ifTrue
 49→
 50→public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 51→    StandardRule(
 52→        id = "function-signature",
 53→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 54→            // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 55→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 56→        ),
 57→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 58→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 59→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 60→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 61→            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 62→            FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    Rule.Experimental {
 66→    private var indent: String? = null
 67→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 68→    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 69→        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 70→    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 71→
 72→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 73→        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[
 74→            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 75→        ]
 76→        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 77→        val indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 78→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 79→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 80→        )
 81→        indent = indentConfig.indent
 82→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 83→    }
 84→
 85→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 86→        node: ASTNode,
 87→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 88→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 89→    ) {
 90→        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 91→            node
 92→                .functionSignatureNodes()
 93→                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 94→                .ifTrue {
 95→                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
 96→                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
 97→                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
 98→                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
 99→                    // effort to attempt it.
100→                    return
101→                }
102→
103→            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
104→        }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
108→        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
109→        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
110→        // or
111→        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
112→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
113→        val startOfBodyBlock =
114→            this
115→                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
116→                ?.firstChildNode
117→        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
118→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
119→            .childrenBetween(
120→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
121→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
122→            )
123→    }
124→
125→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
126→        val funNode = if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
127→            this.treeParent
128→        } else {
129→            this
130→        }
131→        funNode
132→            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
133→            ?.let { modifierList ->
134→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
135→                var currentNode: ASTNode
136→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
137→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
138→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
139→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
140→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
141→                    ) {
142→                        return currentNode
143→                    }
144→                }
145→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
146→            }
147→        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
151→        node: ASTNode,
152→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
153→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154→    ) {
155→        require(node.elementType == FUN)
156→
157→        val forceMultilineSignature =
158→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
159→                node.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine()
160→        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
161→            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162→            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
163→            // like below, which are not acceptable:
164→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
165→            //     ) = "some-value"
166→            //
167→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
168→            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
169→            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
170→            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
171→            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
172→            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
173→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
174→                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
175→                // signature needs to be recalculated
176→                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
177→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
178→            } else {
179→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
180→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
181→            }
182→        } else {
183→            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
184→            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
185→            // signature.
186→            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature = node
187→                .functionSignatureNodes()
188→                .none { it.textContains('\n') }
189→            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
190→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
191→            } else {
192→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
193→            }
194→        }
195→    }
196→
197→    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
198→        val closingParenthesis =
199→            node
200→                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
201→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
202→        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature = node
203→            .functionSignatureNodes()
204→            .childrenBetween(
205→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
206→                endASTNodePredicate = { false },
207→            )
208→
209→        return node.lineIndent().length +
210→            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
211→    }
212→
213→    private fun ASTNode.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine(): Boolean =
214→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
215→            ?.children()
216→            .orEmpty()
217→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
218→            .mapNotNull {
219→                // If the value parameter contains a modifier then this list is followed by a white space
220→                it.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)?.nextSibling { true }
221→            }.any { it.textContains('\n') }
222→
223→    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
224→        node: ASTNode,
225→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
226→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
227→    ): Int {
228→        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
229→
230→        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
231→        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
232→        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
233→            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
234→            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
235→    }
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = lineIndent().length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
238→
239→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
240→        functionSignatureNodes()
241→            .joinTextToString()
242→            .length
243→
244→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
245→        node: ASTNode,
246→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
247→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
248→        multiline: Boolean,
249→        dryRun: Boolean,
250→    ): Int {
251→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
252→
253→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
254→        val firstParameterInList =
255→            valueParameterList
256→                .children()
257→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
258→
259→        whiteSpaceCorrection += if (firstParameterInList == null) {
260→            // handle empty parameter list
261→            fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
262→        } else {
263→            fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
264→                fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
265→                fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
266→        }
267→
268→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
269→    }
270→
271→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
272→        node: ASTNode,
273→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
274→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
275→        dryRun: Boolean,
276→    ): Int {
277→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
278→
279→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
280→
281→        valueParameterList
282→            .children()
283→            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
284→            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
285→                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
286→                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
287→                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
288→            }
289→            .firstOrNull()
290→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
291→                if (!dryRun) {
292→                    emit(
293→                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
294→                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
295→                        true,
296→                    )
297→                }
298→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
299→                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
300→                } else {
301→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
302→                }
303→            }
304→
305→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
306→    }
307→
308→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
309→        node: ASTNode,
310→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
311→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
312→        multiline: Boolean,
313→        dryRun: Boolean,
314→    ): Int {
315→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
316→
317→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
318→        val firstParameterInList =
319→            valueParameterList
320→                .children()
321→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
322→
323→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
324→        firstParameter
325→            ?.prevLeaf()
326→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
327→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
328→                if (multiline) {
329→                    val expectedParameterIndent = "\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent
330→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
331→                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
332→                    ) {
333→                        if (!dryRun) {
334→                            emit(
335→                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
336→                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
337→                                true,
338→                            )
339→                        }
340→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
341→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
342→                        } else {
343→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
344→                        }
345→                    }
346→                } else {
347→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
348→                        if (!dryRun) {
349→                            emit(
350→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
351→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
352→                                true,
353→                            )
354→                        }
355→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
356→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
357→                        } else {
358→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
359→                        }
360→                    }
361→                }
362→            }
363→
364→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
365→    }
366→
367→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
368→        node: ASTNode,
369→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
371→        multiline: Boolean,
372→        dryRun: Boolean,
373→    ): Int {
374→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
375→
376→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
377→        val firstParameterInList =
378→            valueParameterList
379→                .children()
380→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
381→
382→        valueParameterList
383→            .children()
384→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
385→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
386→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
387→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
388→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
389→                    ?.prevLeaf()
390→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
391→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
392→                        if (multiline) {
393→                            val expectedParameterIndent = "\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent
394→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
395→                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
396→                            ) {
397→                                if (!dryRun) {
398→                                    emit(
399→                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
400→                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
401→                                        true,
402→                                    )
403→                                }
404→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
405→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
406→                                } else {
407→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
408→                                }
409→                            }
410→                        } else {
411→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
412→                                if (!dryRun) {
413→                                    emit(
414→                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
415→                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
416→                                        true,
417→                                    )
418→                                }
419→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
420→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
421→                                } else {
422→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
423→                                }
424→                            }
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→            }
428→
429→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
430→    }
431→
432→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
433→        node: ASTNode,
434→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        multiline: Boolean,
437→        dryRun: Boolean,
438→    ): Int {
439→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
440→
441→        val newlineAndIndent = "\n" + node.lineIndent()
442→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
443→
444→        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
445→        closingParenthesis
446→            ?.prevSibling { true }
447→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
448→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
449→                if (multiline) {
450→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
451→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
452→                    ) {
453→                        if (!dryRun) {
454→                            emit(
455→                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
456→                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
457→                                true,
458→                            )
459→                        }
460→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
461→                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
462→                        } else {
463→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
464→                        }
465→                    }
466→                } else {
467→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
468→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
469→                    ) {
470→                        if (!dryRun) {
471→                            emit(
472→                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
473→                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
474→                                true,
475→                            )
476→                        }
477→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
478→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
479→                        } else {
480→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
481→                        }
482→                    }
483→                }
484→            }
485→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
486→    }
487→
488→    private fun fixFunctionBody(
489→        node: ASTNode,
490→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
491→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
492→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
493→    ) {
494→        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
495→            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
496→        } else {
497→            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)
498→        }
499→    }
500→
501→    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
502→        node: ASTNode,
503→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
504→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
505→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
506→    ) {
507→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
508→            node
509→                .findChildByType(EQ)
510→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
511→                ?: return
512→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
513→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
514→        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
515→
516→        val functionBodyExpressionLines = functionBodyExpressionNodes
517→            .joinTextToString()
518→            .split("\n")
519→        functionBodyExpressionLines
520→            .firstOrNull()
521→            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
522→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
523→                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
524→                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
525→                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
526→                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
527→                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
528→                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
529→                                )
530→                        } else {
531→                            false
532→                        }
533→                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
534→                        emit(
535→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
536→                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
537→                            true,
538→                        )
539→                        if (autoCorrect) {
540→                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
541→                        }
542→                    }
543→                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
544→                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
545→                ) {
546→                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
547→                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
548→                    ) {
549→                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
550→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
551→                        ) {
552→                            emit(
553→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
554→                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
555→                                true,
556→                            )
557→                            if (autoCorrect) {
558→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
559→                                    .first()
560→                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
561→                            }
562→                        }
563→                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
564→                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
565→                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
566→                    ) {
567→                        emit(
568→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
569→                            "Newline expected before expression body",
570→                            true,
571→                        )
572→                        if (autoCorrect) {
573→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
574→                                .first()
575→                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent)
576→                        }
577→                    }
578→                }
579→            }
580→    }
581→
582→    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
583→        functionSignatureNodes()
584→            .childrenBetween(
585→                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
586→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
587→            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
588→            .split("\n")
589→            .lastOrNull()
590→            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
591→            ?: false
592→
593→    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
594→        node: ASTNode,
595→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
596→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
597→    ) {
598→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
599→            node
600→                .getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody()
601→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
602→                ?: return
603→
604→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
605→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
606→        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
607→
608→        functionBodyBlock
609→            .joinTextToString()
610→            .split("\n")
611→            .firstOrNull()
612→            ?.also {
613→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
614→                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
615→                    if (autoCorrect) {
616→                        functionBodyBlock
617→                            .first()
618→                            .prevLeaf(true)
619→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
620→                    }
621→                }
622→            }
623→    }
624→
625→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionBody(splitNode: ASTNode?): List<ASTNode> =
626→        this
627→            .collectLeavesRecursively()
628→            .childrenBetween(
629→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == splitNode },
630→                endASTNodePredicate = {
631→                    // collect all remaining nodes
632→                    false
633→                },
634→            )
635→
636→    private fun List<ASTNode>.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull() =
637→        this
638→            .firstOrNull()
639→            ?.takeIf { first -> first.isWhiteSpace() }
640→
641→    private fun isMaxLineLengthSet() = maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
642→
643→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
644→
645→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
646→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
647→            listOf(this)
648→        } else {
649→            children()
650→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
651→                .toList()
652→        }
653→
654→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
655→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
656→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
657→    ): List<ASTNode> {
658→        val iterator = iterator()
659→        var currentNode: ASTNode
660→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
661→
662→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
663→            currentNode = iterator.next()
664→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
665→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
666→                break
667→            }
668→        }
669→
670→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
671→            currentNode = iterator.next()
672→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
673→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
674→                break
675→            }
676→        }
677→
678→        return childrenBetween
679→    }
680→
681→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
682→        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }
683→
684→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
685→
686→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {
687→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
688→        return valueParameterList
689→            .children()
690→            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
691→    }
692→
693→    private fun ASTNode.getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody(): ASTNode? =
694→        this
695→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
696→            ?.firstChildNode
697→            ?.prevCodeLeaf()
698→
699→    public companion object {
700→        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
701→            EditorConfigProperty(
702→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
703→                    "ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
704→                    "Force wrapping the parameters of the function signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
705→                        "number of parameters even in case the entire function signature would fit on a single line. " +
706→                        "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
707→                    PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
708→                    setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
709→                ),
710→                defaultValue = Int.MAX_VALUE,
711→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 2,
712→            )
713→
714→        public val FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping> =
715→            EditorConfigProperty(
716→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
717→                    "ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping",
718→                    "Determines how to wrap the body of function in case it is an expression. Use 'default' " +
719→                        "to wrap the body expression only when the first line of the expression does not fit on the same " +
720→                        "line as the function signature. Use 'multiline' to force wrapping of body expressions that " +
721→                        "consists of multiple line. Use 'always' to force wrapping of body expression always.",
722→                    EnumValueParser(FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping::class.java),
723→                    FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
724→                ),
725→                defaultValue = default,
726→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = multiline,
727→            )
728→
729→        private val INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS = Regex("\\s*\\) =")
730→    }
731→
732→    /**
733→     * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
734→     */
735→    @Suppress("EnumEntryName")
736→    public enum class FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping {
737→        /**
738→         * Keep the first line of the body expression on the same line as the function signature if max line length is
739→         * not exceeded.
740→         */
741→        default,
742→
743→        /**
744→         * Force the body expression to start on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line
745→         * body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
746→         */
747→        multiline,
748→
749→        /**
750→         * Always force the body expression to start on a separate line.
751→         */
752→        always,
753→    }
754→}
755→
756→public val FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionSignatureRule().ruleId

 76→
 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    StandardRule(
 87→        id = "wrapping",
 88→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 89→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92→        ),
 93→    ) {
 94→    private var line = 1
 95→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        line = 1
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129→
130→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132→            return
133→        }
134→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137→        val wrapBlock =
138→            when {
139→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143→                }
144→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
145→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf {
146→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
147→                    }
148→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf {
149→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
150→                    }

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` always removes whitespace before the colon and adds a single space after, which can cause the line to exceed max length. The fix should:
1. Check if the function has parameters (like `FunctionSignatureRule` does)
2. If it doesn't have parameters, check whether applying the spacing would exceed max line length
3. Only apply the fix if it won't exceed max line length

Let me implement the fix:

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 18→
 19→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 20→    StandardRule(
 21→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 22→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY),
 23→    ),
 24→    Rule.Experimental {
 25→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 26→
 27→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 28→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 29→    }
 30→
 31→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 32→        node: ASTNode,
 33→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 34→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 35→    ) {
 36→        node.firstChildNode
 37→        node
 38→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 39→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 40→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 41→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 42→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect, node)
 43→            }
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 47→        node: ASTNode,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 50→    ) {
 51→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 52→        node
 53→            .prevLeaf()
 54→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 55→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 56→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 57→                if (autoCorrect) {
 58→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 59→                }
 60→            }
 61→    }
 62→
 63→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 64→        node: ASTNode,
 65→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        functionNode: ASTNode,
 68→    ) {
 69→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 70→        node
 71→            .nextLeaf()
 72→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 73→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 74→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != " ") {
 75→                    // Check if this is a parameterless function and if the fix would exceed max line length
 76→                    if (isParameterlessFunction(functionNode) && wouldExceedMaxLineLength(node, whiteSpaceAfterColon)) {
 77→                        // Don't fix spacing in this case to avoid violating max-line-length
 78→                        return@let
 79→                    }
 80→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 81→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 82→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 83→                    }
 84→                }
 85→            }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun isParameterlessFunction(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
 89→        val valueParameterList = node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST) ?: return true
 90→        return valueParameterList
 91→            .children()
 92→            .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    private fun wouldExceedMaxLineLength(
 96→        colonNode: ASTNode,
 97→        whiteSpaceAfterColon: ASTNode?,
 98→    ): Boolean {
 99→        if (maxLineLength == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
100→            return false
101→        }
102→
103→        // Calculate the current line length before the fix
104→        val currentLineLength = calculateCurrentLineLength(colonNode, whiteSpaceAfterColon)
105→
106→        // The fix will add 1 space (removing current whitespace and adding a single space)
107→        // So the new line length would be currentLineLength + 1
108→        val newLineLength = currentLineLength + 1
109→
110→        return newLineLength > maxLineLength
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun calculateCurrentLineLength(
114→        colonNode: ASTNode,
115→        whiteSpaceAfterColon: ASTNode?,
116→    ): Int {
117→        // Find the start of the line containing the colon
118→        var currentNode: ASTNode? = colonNode
119→        var lineOffset = colonNode.startOffset
120→
121→        while (currentNode != null) {
122→            val prev = currentNode.prevLeaf(includeEmpty = false)
123→            if (prev == null) {
124→                break
125→            }
126→            if (prev.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && prev.textContains('\n')) {
127→                lineOffset = prev.startOffset + 1 // Start after the newline
128→                break
129→            }
130→            lineOffset = minOf(lineOffset, prev.startOffset)
131→            currentNode = prev
132→        }
133→
134→        // Find the end of the line containing the colon
135→        currentNode = colonNode
136→        var lineEndOffset = colonNode.startOffset + colonNode.textLength
137→
138→        while (currentNode != null) {
139→            val next = currentNode.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = false)
140→            if (next == null) {
141→                break
142→            }
143→            if (next.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && next.textContains('\n')) {
144→                break
145→            }
146→            lineEndOffset = maxOf(lineEndOffset, next.startOffset + next.textLength)
147→            currentNode = next
148→        }
149→
150→        // Find the parent node to get the full text
151→        val rootNode = colonNode.treeParent?.treeParent ?: return 0
152→        val nodeText = rootNode.text
153→
154→        // Calculate line length from the text
155→        val lineStartIndex = lineOffset - rootNode.startOffset
156→        val lineEndIndex = lineEndOffset - rootNode.startOffset
157→
158→        if (lineStartIndex < 0 || lineEndIndex > nodeText.length) {
159→            return 0
160→        }
161→
162→        val lineText = nodeText.substring(lineStartIndex, lineEndIndex)
163→        // Get the last line (in case there are multiple newlines)
164→        val lastLineIndex = lineText.lastIndexOf('\n')
165→        val relevantLineText = if (lastLineIndex >= 0) {
166→            lineText.substring(lastLineIndex + 1)
167→        } else {
168→            lineText
169→        }
170→
171→        return relevantLineText.length
172→    }
173→}
174→
175→public val FUNCTION_RETURN_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule().ruleId
176→

{"file_path":"/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest.kt","content":"package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Test that function-return-type-spacing honors max-line-length for parameterless functions.
 * See issue: experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
 */
class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest {
    private val functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Given a parameterless function that would exceed max line length with spacing fix, then do not reformat`() {
        // This is a long function name that would exceed max line length (120) when the colon spacing is corrected
        val code =
            """
                fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
                    AndAlsoALongReturnType {
                }
            """.trimIndent()
        // The rule should not modify the spacing because it would exceed max line length
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a function with parameters that would exceed max line length with spacing fix, then reformat`() {
        // For functions with parameters, the rule should still apply (as per FunctionSignatureRule behavior)
        val code =
            """
                fun foo(param: String):String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(param: String): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 14, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a parameterless function that does not exceed max line length, then reformat`() {
        // Short parameterless function should be reformatted normally
        val code =
            """
                fun foo():String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given whitespace before colon in parameterless function, then remove it`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo() :String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Unexpected whitespace")
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given multiple spaces after colon in parameterless function, then reformat to single space`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo():  String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a newline before colon in parameterless function, then reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo()
                    : String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Unexpected whitespace")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a newline after colon in parameterless function, then reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo():
                    String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}"}

import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Test that function-return-type-spacing honors max-line-length for parameterless functions.
 * See issue: experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
 */
class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest {
    private val functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Given a parameterless function that would exceed max line length with spacing fix, then do not reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
                    AndAlsoALongReturnType {
                }
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a function with parameters that would exceed max line length with spacing fix, then reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo(param: String):String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(param: String): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 14, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a parameterless function that does not exceed max line length, then reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo():String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given whitespace before colon in parameterless function, then remove it`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo() :String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Unexpected whitespace")
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given multiple spaces after colon in parameterless function, then reformat to single space`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo():  String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a newline before colon in parameterless function, then reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo()
                    : String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 9, "Unexpected whitespace")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a newline after colon in parameterless function, then reformat`() {
        val code =
            """
                fun foo():
                    String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
                fun foo(): String = "result"
            """.trimIndent()
        functionReturnTypeSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 10, "Single space expected between colon and return type")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}"}

101→        // Calculate the current line length before the fix
102→        val currentLineLength = calculateCurrentLineLength(colonNode)
103→
104→        // The fix will add 1 space (removing current whitespace and adding a single space)
105→        // So the new line length would be currentLineLength + 1
106→        val newLineLength = currentLineLength + 1
107→
108→        return newLineLength > maxLineLength
109→    }
110→
111→    private fun calculateCurrentLineLength(colonNode: ASTNode): Int {
112→        // Find the start of the line containing the colon
113→        var currentNode: ASTNode? = colonNode
114→        var lineOffset = colonNode.startOffset
115→
116→        while (currentNode != null) {
117→            val prev = currentNode.prevLeaf(includeEmpty = false)
118→            if (prev == null) {
119→                break
120→            }
121→            if (prev.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && prev.textContains('\n')) {
122→                lineOffset = prev.startOffset + 1 // Start after the newline
123→                break
124→            }
125→            lineOffset = minOf(lineOffset, prev.startOffset)
126→            currentNode = prev
127→        }
128→
129→        // Find the end of the line containing the colon
130→        currentNode = colonNode
131→        var lineEndOffset = colonNode.startOffset + colonNode.textLength
132→
133→        while (currentNode != null) {
134→            val next = currentNode.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = false)
135→            if (next == null) {
136→                break
137→            }
138→            if (next.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && next.textContains('\n')) {
139→                break
140→            }
141→            lineEndOffset = maxOf(lineEndOffset, next.startOffset + next.textLength)
142→            currentNode = next
143→        }
144→
145→        // Find the parent node to get the full text
146→        val rootNode = colonNode.treeParent?.treeParent ?: return 0
147→        val nodeText = rootNode.text
148→
149→        // Calculate line length from the text
150→        val lineStartIndex = lineOffset - rootNode.startOffset
151→        val lineEndIndex = lineEndOffset - rootNode.startOffset
152→
153→        if (lineStartIndex < 0 || lineEndIndex > nodeText.length) {
154→            return 0
155→        }
156→
157→        val lineText = nodeText.substring(lineStartIndex, lineEndIndex)
158→        // Get the last line (in case there are multiple newlines)
159→        val lastLineIndex = lineText.lastIndexOf('\n')
160→        val relevantLineText = if (lastLineIndex >= 0) {
161→            lineText.substring(lastLineIndex + 1)
162→        } else {
163→            lineText
164→        }
165→
166→        return relevantLineText.length
167→    }
168→}
169→
170→public val FUNCTION_RETURN_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule().ruleId

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.findChildByType
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lineIndent
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.ifTrue
 22→
 23→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY),
 27→    ),
 28→    Rule.Experimental {
 29→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 30→
 31→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 32→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 33→    }
 34→
 35→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 36→        node: ASTNode,
 37→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 38→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 39→    ) {
 40→        node.firstChildNode
 41→        node
 42→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 43→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 44→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 45→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 46→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect, node)
 47→            }
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 53→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 54→    ) {
 55→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 56→        node
 57→            .prevLeaf()
 58→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 59→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 60→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 61→                if (autoCorrect) {
 62→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 63→                }
 64→            }
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 68→        node: ASTNode,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        functionNode: ASTNode,
 72→    ) {
 73→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 74→        node
 75→            .nextLeaf()
 76→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 77→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 78→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != " ") {
 79→                    // Check if this is a parameterless function and if the fix would exceed max line length
 80→                    if (isParameterlessFunction(functionNode) && wouldExceedMaxLineLength(node, whiteSpaceAfterColon)) {
 81→                        // Don't fix spacing in this case to avoid violating max-line-length
 82→                        return@let
 83→                    }
 84→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 85→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 86→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 87→                    }
 88→                }
 89→            }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun isParameterlessFunction(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
 93→        val valueParameterList = node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST) ?: return true
 94→        return valueParameterList
 95→            .children()
 96→            .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun wouldExceedMaxLineLength(
100→        colonNode: ASTNode,
101→        whiteSpaceAfterColon: ASTNode?,
102→    ): Boolean {
103→        if (maxLineLength == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
104→            return false
105→        }
106→
107→        val isCurrentMultiline = (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text ?: "").contains("\n")
108→
109→        return if (isCurrentMultiline) {
110→            // Current format has newline, converting to single line
111→            // Get function name and calculate if single line would exceed max
112→            val sigLength = getSignatureLengthBeforeColon(colonNode)
113→            val returnTypeNode = whiteSpaceAfterColon?.nextLeaf { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
114→            val returnTypeLength = returnTypeNode?.text?.length ?: 0
115→
116→            // Format would be: "fun name(): ReturnType" (colon + space = 2 chars)
117→            val singleLineLength = sigLength + 2 + returnTypeLength
118→
119→            singleLineLength > maxLineLength
120→        } else {
121→            // Already on single line, adding space won't increase length significantly
122→            false
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun getSignatureLengthBeforeColon(colonNode: ASTNode): Int {
127→        val rootNode = colonNode.treeParent?.treeParent ?: return 0
128→        val nodeText = rootNode.text
129→
130→        val colonIndex = colonNode.startOffset - rootNode.startOffset
131→        if (colonIndex < 0 || colonIndex >= nodeText.length) {
132→            return 0
133→        }
134→
135→        // Find the start of the line from colon
136→        var startIndex = colonIndex
137→        while (startIndex > 0 && nodeText[startIndex - 1] != '\n') {
138→            startIndex--
139→        }
140→
141→        return colonIndex - startIndex
142→    }
143→
144→    companion object {
145→        private val VALUE_PARAMETER = ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
146→        private val VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST = ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
147→    }
148→}
149→
150→public val FUNCTION_RETURN_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule().ruleId
151→
